title: Fleischmannia prasiifolia
taxonomy: Fleischmannia prasiifolia is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. Within the Asteraceae family, Fleischmannia prasiifolia is further classified in the tribe Eupatorieae. The species is also sometimes placed in the subtribe Fleischmanniinae. The exact classification of Fleischmannia prasiifolia is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.
uses: Fleischmannia prasiifolia, a plant species native to Central and South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
